headers.//: Assets are unversioned, so a cached api-config.js can outlive the deploy that added an endpoint it must have. Hosting does not honour If-None-Match here (it answers 200, not 304), so this re-downloads these small files on each load — worth it to never serve stale app code. Revisit if assets get build-hashed. cleanUrls serves pages extensionless, so this has to be a catch-all rather than **/*.html.
